titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Matthew Jennett Papers
titlestmt.author: by Michael L. Forstrom

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 26, 2018 at 05:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Matthew Jennett papers contain subject files, sound recordings, and other material documenting the life and work of Matthew Jennett as editor of Pharos Books. The subject files feature correspondence, writings, and printed ephemera relating to publication projects with Pharos Books. There are files for authors, publishers, and designers, including Bob Cato, Franz Douskey, Peter Ganick (of Potes & Poets Press), Leslie Lee, and Jonathan Williams (of the Jargon Society), among others. File also contain examples of fine printing by small presses such as the Ives Street Press. Sound recordings collected by Jennett consist of nineteen 78 speed albums containing radio broadcasts dating from the 1940s. There are programs from the National Broadcasting Company (NBC) and the Columbia Workshop, including "Emma Lazarus," from the "Eternal Light Series" held at the Jewish Theological Seminary in New York City, and "They Burned the Books," a radio drama written by Stephen Vincent Benét.

langmaterial.language: Chiefly in English; some materials in Greek.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 6-9 (sound recordings): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Matthew Jennett Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Matthew Jennett Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was acquired through a series of gifts from Matthew Jennett. The original gift, consisting of subject files, was received in 2006 and cataloged as the Matthew Jennett Papers Relating to Pharos Books (YCAL MSS 231). Subsequent gifts of manuscript material received in 2007 and 2008, consisting of additional subject files, sound recordings, and other material, were added to the papers and the collection was renamed the Matthew Jennett Papers.

bioghist.head: Matthew Jennett
bioghist.p: Matthew Jennett, former editor of Pharos Books in New Haven, Connecticut, is Curator of Special Collections, Rare Books & Archives, The American College of Greece.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Matthew Jennett papers contain subject files, sound recordings, and other material documenting the life and work of Matthew Jennett as editor of Pharos Books. The subject files feature correspondence, writings, printed ephemera, such as broadside and keepsakes, and other material documenting publication projects with Pharos Books. There are files for authors, publishers, and designers, including Bob Cato, Franz Douskey, Peter Ganick, Leslie Lee, and Jonathan Williams, among others. Sound recordings collected by Jennett consist of nineteen 78 speed albums containing radio broadcasts dating from the 1940s. There are programs from the National Broadcasting Company (NBC) and the Columbia Workshop, including "Emma Lazarus," from the "Eternal Light Series" held at the Jewish Theological Seminary in New York City, and "They Burned the Books," a radio drama written by Stephen Vincent Benet.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: Series I. Subject Files, 1883-2000. Series II. Sound Recordings, 1940-1949. Series III. Other Material, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I, Subject Files, features correspondence, writings, printed ephemera, and other material documenting publication projects with Pharos Books. There are files for authors, small presses, publishers, and designers, including Bob Cato, Franz Douskey, Peter Ganick, Leslie Lee, and Jonathan Williams, among others.

Folders have been preserved in their alphabetical arrangment by personal name. This means that files associated with small presses or publishers are arranged by the individual's last name. Hence, material relating to Potes & Poets Press can be found with Ganick, Peter, and files relating to the Jargon Society can be found with Williams, Jonathan. Cross references are provided where appropriate in the box and folder list below.

Many files contain mixed contents, with letters, drafts and proofs, financial records, and other material. Writings, when present, are identified in folder notes. Files also contain examples of modern fine printing by small presses such as Ives Street Press.

did.physloc: See: Douskey, Franz, Box 1, folders 6-10

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proofs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes third-party letters from Lola Ridge, editor of Broom , to "Stephen"

did.physloc: See: Douskey, Franz, Box 1, folders 6-10
See: Taylor, Alexander, Box 4, folder 49

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Anthoensen Press, Malcolm Cowley, The Ingram Merrill Foundation, and others, as well as drafts for Archeological Nights and other writings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph by Roberto Masotti

did.physloc: Also stored in: Oversize, Box 5, folder 60

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed version of Fife's Portraits From Memory: New Orleans in the Sixties (Surregional Press) and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proofs of Ganick's Remove a Concept

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains copy of "Declaration of Three..."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts of Head's The Green Tree and other poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes corrected draft of Holmes' essay "The Huckleberry Party"

did.physloc: See: Douskey, Franz, Box 1, folders 6-10
See: Cash, Barbara, Box 1, folder 4
See: William, Jonathan, Box 4, folders 51-54

"Almos' a Man" (filmscript)
"Between Now and Then" (playscript)
"The Book of Lambert" (playscript)
"Killing Time" (playscript)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes drafts:
"Never the Time and the Place" (novel)
"Solomon Northrup: Twelve Years a Slave" (teleplay)
"Willie" (playscript)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes third-party letters relating to William Rose Benet

scopecontent.p: Includes printed version of Shaw's Private Time, Public Time (1969)

scopecontent.p: Includes printed versions of Smith's Concerning the Education of a Print Collector

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by program title. Titles taken from external labels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II, Sound Recordings, consists of nineteen 78 speed albums containing radio broadcasts dating from the 1940s. There are programs from the National Broadcasting Company (NBC) and the Columbia Workshop, including "Emma Lazarus," from the "Eternal Light Series" held at the Jewish Theological Seminary in New York City, and "They Burned the Books," a radio drama written by Stephen Vincent Benet.
Albums are restricted pending reformatting and may be consulted only with persmission of the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 6-9 (sound recordings): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III, Other Material, consists of a small number of "miscellaneous" items.
